raw_consumer_statement:
I am an active-duty XXXX military XXXX currently stationed in XXXX. I do not have access to XXXX XXXX services from my overseas location. On XX/XX/year>, a charge of {$30.00} appeared on my Platinum Card from XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, WA XXXX. 

I did not recognize this charge and did not authorize it. Because I am stationed overseas and can not access XXXX XXXX services, I have no basis on which this charge could be legitimate. 

I opened a dispute through the American Express app. The dispute was assigned case number XXXX with a stated reason of " transaction not recognized. '' The app indicated the expected resolution date was XX/XX/year>, the same day the dispute was logged, which is the same day I checked the status. The status at that time read " Investigation in progress. '' American Express subsequently closed the dispute without providing me any substantive written explanation, findings, or basis for closure. I received no notification of outcome, no provisional credit, and no opportunity to provide additional supporting documentation before the case was closed. 

Under Regulation Z and the Fair Credit Billing Act, American Express is required to conduct a reasonable investigation of disputed charges and provide written notice of its findings. Closing a dispute without substantive communication, particularly where the cardholder is an overseas military servicemember with no access to the merchant 's services, fails to meet that standard. 

I am requesting that American Express reopen dispute XXXX, conduct a proper investigation, issue a provisional credit pending resolution, and provide written findings as required by law. I am also requesting that the CFPB review whether American Express is systematically closing disputes without adequate investigation or required written notice to cardmembers.
